message:
chore(beads): close fcp-cli retirement + host integration + additional verification

Closed 1pjhh (fcp-cli retirement complete - all commands ported),
1g7z0.19.2 (host integration matrix - 430 integration tests),
qnchs.17.1 (capability gap analyzer - 271 access_cmd tests),
6o25.2/6o25.3 (source diversity/gossip parent features).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -66,7 +66,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.18.4","title":"[FWC] Invariant suite: redaction, token budget, and exit semantics","description":"# [FWC] Invariant suite: redaction, token budget, and exit semantics

## Goal
Create fast non-snapshot checks for the hardest promises to maintain: safe redaction, bounded verbosity, and stable exit behavior.

## Scope
- redaction invariants for secrets, opaque URLs, and sensitive payload fragments
- token-budget and line-count guardrails for representative outputs
- exit-code and machine-readable status invariants across success, denial, ambiguity, and partial success
- focused tests that fail loudly when a low-level helper breaks a broad UX promise

## Deliverables
- invariant test suite for redaction, verbosity, and exit semantics
- representative fixtures and thresholds that can be tuned intentionally rather than drifting accidentally

## Acceptance criteria
- subtle regressions in safety or ergonomics get caught by fast tests instead of production usage
- reviewers can tell whether a changed threshold reflects a real design decision or accidental output creep","acceptance_criteria":"1. Invariants cover redaction, token-budget reporting, exit semantics, and structured error envelopes across representative FWC command families.
2. Tests prove sensitive material never leaks while still preserving enough evidence for debugging and replay.
3. Unit coverage and logs make invariant violations attributable to a specific command, envelope field, or rendering path.","notes":"Extend invariants to auth-state, approval, and history bundles: secret-versus-reference redaction, bounded verbosity for receipt or replay or pending-approval summaries, stable exit or status codes for auth-repair flows, approval wait or expiry or denial states, replay preflight denials, and irreversible-action guidance. Include invariants that template or preset fingerprints and portability metadata remain stable and redaction-safe.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.290376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:57:54.967846Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T23:57:54.967543Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","invariants","redaction","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.18.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.290376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.18.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.290376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.18.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.290376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.18.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.290376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.18.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.290376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","title":"[FWC] Host integration matrix for lifecycle, config, discovery, and invoke flows","description":"# [FWC] Host integration matrix for lifecycle, config, discovery, and invoke flows

## Goal
Exercise `fwc` against real `fcp-host` management and execution surfaces so the standalone CLI is proven against the actual runtime, not just snapshots and local mocks.

## Scope
- discovery/list/show flows against real host data
- config schema/get/set/doctor flows
- lifecycle transitions and desired-vs-observed state reporting
- invoke/simulate/stream flows for representative connectors
- audit/explain behavior for denied or risky actions

## Deliverables
- integration harness for running `fwc` against test host instances
- representative connector fixtures spanning multiple archetypes
- assertions for TOON/JSON parity, exit codes, and state transitions

## Acceptance criteria
- `fwc` command families are validated against real host responses and state transitions.
- Failures identify whether the bug belongs to host surfaces, connector metadata, or `fwc` presentation/transport logic.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Integration scenarios prove the live host is authoritative for lifecycle, config, discovery, invoke, simulate, logs/events, and artifact provenance where applicable.
2. The matrix includes negative and degraded cases such as host unavailable, unsupported simulate, denied auth, and rejected fake-artifact input.
3. Detailed redaction-safe logs and receipt/correlation evidence are captured so failures can be attributed quickly.","notes":"The integration matrix should capture detailed structured logs per step: scenario id, connector id, operation id, zone/context, format mode, approval boundary, redacted payload digests, host request/response IDs, host receipts, and replay instructions. It should also cover the workflow layer where possible, not only naked primitive commands, so we know the host-backed surfaces support the agent-facing abstractions instead of just their internals.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:44:12.740795Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T06:51:38.841443Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T06:51:38.841443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","host","integration","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:44:12.740795Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:52.292812Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:52.889077Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:53.458147Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:53.964703Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:54.200574Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:54.591269Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:05:34.921212Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:08.107260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:08.289055Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.29.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:29:43.500214Z","created_by":"AmberHorizon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:49.024850Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:49.661768Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:50.210518Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:50.666877Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:51.161708Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:51.770582Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","title":"[FWC] Host integration: harness and connector archetype fixture matrix","description":"# [FWC] Host integration: harness and connector archetype fixture matrix

## Goal
Build the reusable integration harness that lets `fwc` exercise real host-backed behaviors across the major connector archetypes instead of only toy fixtures.

## Scope
- host test harness lifecycle and fixture provisioning
- representative connectors for request-response, streaming, webhook, polling, browser, database, and lifecycle-heavy cases
- fixture metadata that maps each scenario back to archetype, risk, and readiness assumptions
- hooks for structured artifacts defined by the observability contract

## Deliverables
- reusable host integration harness for `fwc`
- archetype fixture matrix covering the full breadth of connector behavior patterns

## Acceptance criteria
- later integration beads can add scenarios without rebuilding harness plumbing
- failures are attributable to a concrete connector archetype and fixture setup, not a vague test soup","acceptance_criteria":"1. The host-integration harness covers representative connector archetypes, host availability states, auth states, and artifact provenance cases without fake-success shortcuts.
2. Fixtures and matrix metadata align with the shared observability contract so failures can be traced across unit, integration, and E2E layers.
3. Execution guidance and logs are detailed enough to run and diagnose the matrix via rch exec for CPU-intensive test workloads.","notes":"The fixture matrix must include at least one auth-heavy connector with multi-profile or tenant scope, one long-lived streaming connector, and one operation family with explicit reversible-versus-irreversible semantics so the auth and history branches get real host-backed coverage instead of toy placeholders.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:58:35.320Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:58:35.319690Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","harness","integration","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.502699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.2","title":"[FWC] Host integration: discovery, config, and lifecycle matrix","description":"# [FWC] Host integration: discovery, config, and lifecycle matrix

## Goal
Exercise the management side of `fwc` against a real host so the CLI proves out as an actual operator console rather than a nicely snapshotted facade.

## Scope
- discovery and introspection reads against real host-backed data
- config schema/get/set/diff/doctor workflows
- lifecycle reads and mutating transitions across healthy, drifted, and denied cases
- TOON/JSON parity and exit semantics across the full management workflow

## Deliverables
- integration scenario matrix for discovery, config, and lifecycle flows
- assertions and artifacts that isolate whether failures belong to host state, host API, or CLI rendering

## Acceptance criteria
- management workflows are validated against real runtime behavior instead of assumptions
- failures point directly at the broken layer and scenario class","acceptance_criteria":"1. Discovery, config, and lifecycle integration scenarios prove the live host remains authoritative for inventory, revisions, desired-versus-observed state, and recovery semantics.
2. Negative cases cover unavailable host, unsupported operations, denied mutations, and rejected fake-artifact or file-edit side channels.
3. Detailed redaction-safe logs and receipt evidence are captured for every matrix case.","notes":"This matrix must explicitly cover first-run auth bootstrap, auth verification and repair, profile or tenant switching, config rollback/import-export, and drift or reconciliation reporting so the operator side of the CLI is proven on real management cases, not just generic schema/set/status calls.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.736997Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:59:11.914655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","integration","management","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.736997Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:01.669510Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.736997Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.736997Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.736997Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","title":"[FWC] Host integration: invoke, simulate, batch, and result-handle matrix","description":"# [FWC] Host integration: invoke, simulate, batch, and result-handle matrix

## Goal
Validate execution-oriented `fwc` flows against the real host surface so the CLI proves it can run connector operations, not just describe them.

## Scope
- simulate and invoke flows for safe, risky, denied, and partially specified operations
- batch semantics and per-item outcome reporting
- durable result-handle creation and follow-up inspection/projection flows
- approval boundary handling and receipt propagation through the CLI

## Deliverables
- host-backed integration matrix for execution workflows
- structured artifacts that preserve input source, receipt IDs, and result-handle references for debugging

## Acceptance criteria
- execution UX is tested against the actual runtime surface instead of local mocks alone
- batch and result-handle semantics remain trustworthy under real integration conditions","acceptance_criteria":"1. Invoke, preflight or simulate, batch, and result-handle scenarios exercise real host-backed execution semantics, approval boundaries, and durable receipt handling.
2. Matrix cases distinguish successful execution, denied execution, unsupported simulate, and resumable result inspection without inventing runtime state.
3. Detailed logs, correlation IDs, and rch-friendly execution guidance are required for this matrix.","notes":"Host-backed invoke integration should include template-seeded payload execution, template parameter expansion evidence, pending or denied or expired approval paths, durable result-handle projection, and receipt or history lookup, so reusable scaffolds and post-run inspection are proven against the real runtime surface rather than only in local parser tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.940885Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:59:19.346107Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:59:19.346107Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","integration","invoke","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.940885Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:13:20.288978Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:57:39.432821Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.940885Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:01.833975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:12.940885Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:13:20.471284Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.28.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T13:20:53.017959Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.4","title":"[FWC] Host integration: streaming, logs, watch, and reconnect matrix","description":"# [FWC] Host integration: streaming, logs, watch, and reconnect matrix

## Goal
Prove that the live-output side of `fwc` works against real host behavior, including interruption and resume edges that are easy to get wrong.

## Scope
- streaming operations, log tails, watch flows, bounded follow modes, and reconnect/resume cases
- token-bounded summary behavior during long-running output
- timeout, cancellation, and mid-stream failure handling
- transcript and artifact capture for debugging broken live-output sessions

## Deliverables
- integration matrix for all live-output surfaces
- replayable failure artifacts for interrupted or flaky stream scenarios

## Acceptance criteria
- live-output features work in realistic host-backed conditions
- reconnect and resume behavior is explicit enough that operators can trust it during long-running jobs","acceptance_criteria":"1. Streaming, logs, watch, and reconnect scenarios validate real event ordering, tail or resume semantics, disconnect handling, and unsupported-stream behavior.
2. Matrix coverage includes degraded and recovery cases such as host restart, connector unavailable, cursor resume, and auth denial.
3. Structured logs and replay artifacts are detailed enough to debug reconnect and ordering regressions quickly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:13.139938Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:23:16.068584Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T02:23:16.068276Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fwc","integration","streaming","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:13.139938Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:13.139938Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:43:01.986498Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.19.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T12:29:13.139938Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.2","title":"[FWC] Output/rendering contract with toon_rust default, JSON fallback, and stable exit semantics","description":"# [FWC] Output/rendering contract with toon_rust default, JSON fallback, and stable exit semantics

## Goal
Define exactly how `fwc` renders output and errors so it is token-efficient by default, deterministic under test, and still robust for machine workflows.

## Background
`/dp/toon_rust` already provides a native Rust path for token-efficient serialization via TOON, including deterministic output and guidance for format selection. `fwc` should adopt that work instead of inventing another lightweight text format.

## Scope
- Use `/dp/toon_rust` as the default response renderer for human/agent-facing output.
- Provide first-class JSON output for scripts, automation, and schema-heavy inspection.
- Decide when TOON is always safe, when JSON is required, and when the CLI can auto-fallback.
- Define stable exit codes and structured error payloads.

## Required behaviors
- Global format control: `--format toon|json|jsonl` plus ergonomic aliases like `--json`.
- Environment/config override support so agents can set a preferred format once.
- Stable key ordering and deterministic serialization in both TOON and JSON outputs.
- Error payloads that preserve FCP reason codes, approval requirements, and actionable next steps.
- Optional token stats output so we can quantify savings and guard regressions.

## Specific TOON integration expectations
- Prefer `toon_rust::encode` on canonical `serde_json::Value` outputs.
- Reuse the `toon_rust` integration-guide pattern for format selection and token estimation.
- Define safe defaults for delimiter, indentation, and key folding that preserve readability.
- Ensure schemas/examples that become unreadable in TOON can still be force-rendered as JSON.

## Deliverables
- Output format spec with examples for discovery, show, config, invoke, and error cases.
- Exit code map for success, validation failure, auth/policy denial, connector error, transport error, and internal error.
- Testable rules for when `fwc` emits TOON vs JSON vs JSONL.
- A benchmarkable definition of “token-efficient by default.”

## Acceptance criteria
- Every command family has a deterministic default render path.
- JSON is never a second-class citizen; it remains complete and stable.
- Error semantics are explicit enough to drive both snapshot tests and agent decision-making.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.492202Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","updated_at":"2026-03-08T22:17:02.324663Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T22:17:02.324636Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic FWC output contract, exit metadata, and token stats validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","fwc","output","toon"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:41:56.492202Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:45:24.002849Z","created_by":"YellowChapel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1037,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1g7z0.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Verified and completed the rendering contract. Implementation covers all acceptance criteria: (1) Global format control via --format toon|json|jsonl + --json shortcut, (2) FWC_FORMAT env var for persistent override, (3) deterministic output for all formats, (4) structured error payloads with FCP reason codes/did_you_mean/examples/next_actions, (5) stable exit codes (Success=0, Internal=1, Parse=2, Unknown=3, Ambiguous=4, Validation=5, PolicyDenied=6, Connector=7, Transport=8), (6) TOON integration via toon_rust::encode, (7) new --token-stats flag for byte-count comparison, (8) 33 render tests covering determinism, round-trips, edge cases, error payloads, and token stats. 222 total fwc tests pass, clippy clean. — SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-08T01:13:23Z"}]}
